nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: 撒哈拉以南非洲
nodes.summary: 撒哈拉以南非洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联90种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、埃及雁、Western crested guinea fowl等90种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 灌丛
nodes.summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 古勒姆特区
nodes.summary: 古勒姆特区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Calitzdorp
nodes.summary: Calitzdorp是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 臺拉維夫大學
nodes.summary: 臺拉維夫大學是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鹳科
nodes.summary: 鹳科（Ciconiidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 白鹳
nodes.summary: 白鹳（White stork，学名 Ciconia ciconia），分类上属于鹳形目，并归入鹳科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联8个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括灌丛、稀树草原、湿地，主要威胁包括农药污染、非法猎捕、猎捕压力等4项。

nodes.name: 布雷達斯多普
nodes.summary: 布雷達斯多普是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鹳形目
nodes.summary: 鹳形目（Ciconiiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联17种鸟类，如钳嘴鹳、非洲秃鹳、Greater adjutant等17种鸟类。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 南非
nodes.summary: 南非是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联195种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、非洲黑鴨、Shelley's francolin等195种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 切尔诺贝利核事故
nodes.summary: 切尔诺贝利核事故是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 中世纪
nodes.summary: 中世纪是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括白鹳，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white stork is a long-distance migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical Sub-Saharan Africa to as far south as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The white stork is a long-distance migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical Sub-Saharan Africa to as far south as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Some populations adhere to the eastern migration route, which passes across Israel into eastern and central Africa. In Africa the white stork may spend the winter in Tunisia, Morocco, Uganda, Angola, Zimbabwe, Djibouti, Botswana, Mozambique, Zambia, Eswatini, Gambia, Guinea, Algeria, and Ghana. A few records of breeding from South Africa have been known since 1933 at Calitzdorp, and about 10 birds have been known to breed since the 1990s around Bredasdorp.{{cite book |pages= 82–3 |title= The Atlas of Southern African Birds |volume=1: Non-passerines |last= Allan |first= David G

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |publisher= BirdLife South Africa |isbn= 978-0-620-20729-4 |chapter-url= http://sabap2.adu.org.za/docs/sabap1/083.pdf |via= South African Bird Atlas Project 2}} A small population of white storks winters in India and is thought to derive principally from the C. c. asiatica population as flocks of up to 200 birds have been observed on spring migration in the early 1900s through the Kurram Valley.{{cite journal |last1= Whitehead |first1= Charles Hughes Tempest |year= 1911 |title= On the birds of Kohat and the Kurram Valley

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It avoids areas overgrown with tall grass and shrubs. In the Chernobyl area of northern Ukraine, white stork populations declined after the 1986 nuclear accident, as farmland was succeeded by tall grass and shrubs. In parts of Poland, poor natural foraging grounds have forced birds to seek food at rubbish dumps since 1999. White storks have also been reported foraging in rubbish dumps in the Middle East, North Africa and South Africa. Anthropogenic litter was found in the pellets of one third of breeding pairs in Poland, even though all pairs nested far from major dumps and landfills. The white stork breeds in greater numbers in areas with open grasslands, particularly grassy areas which are wet or periodically flooded, and less in areas with taller vegetation cover such as forest and shrubland. They make use of grasslands, wetlands, and farmland on the wintering grounds in Africa. White storks were probably aided by human activities during the Middle Ages as woodland was cleared and new pastures and farmland were created, and they were found across much of Europe, breeding as far north as Sweden

links.label: 保护等级
links.evidence: status = LC

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The plan is intended to address key issues such as species and habitat conservation, management of human activities, research, education, and implementation. Threats include the continued loss of wetlands, collisions with overhead power lines, use of persistent pesticides (such as DDT) to combat locusts in Africa, and largely illegal hunting on migration routes and wintering grounds. [[File:Ciconia ciconia juv small.jpg|thumb|left|alt=Three long-legged, long-billed black and white birds stand on a huge pile of sticks atop an artificial platform on a pole|A nest on a man-made platform in Poland

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Afterwards, it began declining mainly due to habitat loss (especially conversion of wetlands and meadows into modern farming), with only a few tens of breeding pairs in 1974 and none in 2008. Since then, it has reestablished itself and the population has slowly started to increase, reaching ten pairs in 2023. In Armenia, the population of the white stork slightly increased in the period between 2005 and 2015, and by the last data reached 652 pairs.Aghababyan K
